sections.title: SKILLS 11-13

parts.title: SKILL 11: LISTEN FOR EXPRESSIONS OF AGREEMENT
parts.instruction: In this exercise, listen carefully to each short dialogue and question on the recording, and then choose the best answer to the question. You should pay attention to expressions of agreement.

questions.passage:
(man) I'd like to take a trip down the coast this weekend.
(woman) Me, too.
questions.question: 1. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The trip would cost too much.

reason.en: The woman's statement 'Me, too' indicates agreement with the man's desire to take a trip, not a concern about cost.
reason.id: Pernyataan wanita 'Me, too' menunjukkan persetujuan dengan keinginan pria untuk melakukan perjalanan, bukan kekhawatiran tentang biaya.

options.text: She doesn't think that a trip would be a good idea.

reason.en: The phrase 'Me, too' is an expression of agreement, directly contradicting the idea that she doesn't think the trip is a good idea.
reason.id: Frasa 'Me, too' adalah ekspresi persetujuan, yang secara langsung bertentangan dengan gagasan bahwa dia tidak berpikir perjalanan itu ide yang bagus.

options.text: She would like to take two trips rather than one.

reason.en: The woman is agreeing with the man's desire for one trip, not expressing a preference for two trips.
reason.id: Wanita itu setuju dengan keinginan pria untuk satu perjalanan, bukan mengungkapkan preferensi untuk dua perjalanan.

options.text: She would also like to take a trip.

reason.en: The phrase 'Me, too' is a common expression of agreement, meaning she shares the same desire as the man to take a trip.
reason.id: Frasa 'Me, too' adalah ekspresi persetujuan yang umum, yang berarti dia memiliki keinginan yang sama dengan pria itu untuk melakukan perjalanan.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement

questions.passage:
(woman) I would like to see Matt elected to the town council next month.
(man) So would I.
questions.question: 2. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He would like to see the elections for town council.

reason.en: The man's statement 'So would I' indicates agreement with the woman's desire for Matt to be elected, not a general interest in the elections themselves.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria 'So would I' menunjukkan persetujuan dengan keinginan wanita agar Matt terpilih, bukan ketertarikan umum pada pemilihan itu sendiri.

options.text: He agrees that Matt should be elected.

reason.en: The phrase 'So would I' is a common expression of agreement in English, directly mirroring the woman's sentiment that Matt should be elected.
reason.id: Frasa 'So would I' adalah ungkapan persetujuan yang umum dalam bahasa Inggris, yang secara langsung mencerminkan sentimen wanita bahwa Matt harus terpilih.

options.text: He thinks the elections should take place next month.

reason.en: While the woman mentions 'next month,' the man's agreement is specifically about Matt's election, not the timing of the elections in general.
reason.id: Meskipun wanita menyebutkan 'bulan depan', persetujuan pria secara khusus adalah tentang pemilihan Matt, bukan tentang waktu pemilihan secara umum.

options.text: He disagrees with the woman.

reason.en: The phrase 'So would I' is an expression of agreement, directly contradicting the idea that the man disagrees with the woman.
reason.id: Frasa 'So would I' adalah ungkapan persetujuan, yang secara langsung bertentangan dengan gagasan bahwa pria tersebut tidak setuju dengan wanita itu.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement

questions.passage:
(man) I'm not sure if I should take begin ning or intermediate French next semester.
(woman) Neither am I.
questions.question: 3. WHAT DOES THE W7OMAN MEAN?

options.text: She is not sure which course she should take.

reason.en: The woman uses the expression 'Neither am I' to agree with the man's statement of uncertainty about which French course to take. This directly means she shares his uncertainty.
reason.id: Wanita tersebut menggunakan ekspresi 'Neither am I' untuk menyetujui pernyataan ketidakpastian pria tentang kursus bahasa Prancis mana yang harus diambil. Ini secara langsung berarti dia berbagi ketidakpastiannya.

options.text: She's not sure if she should take a trip to France.

reason.en: The dialogue is about choosing French courses, not about taking a trip to France. The phrase 'Neither am I' is an agreement with the previous statement, not a statement about travel plans.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut adalah tentang memilih kursus bahasa Prancis, bukan tentang melakukan perjalanan ke Prancis. Frasa 'Neither am I' adalah persetujuan dengan pernyataan sebelumnya, bukan pernyataan tentang rencana perjalanan.

options.text: She knows that she is not ready for intermediate French.

reason.en: The woman's statement 'Neither am I' indicates she shares the man's uncertainty about *both* beginning and intermediate French, not that she knows she's not ready for intermediate.
reason.id: Pernyataan wanita 'Neither am I' menunjukkan bahwa dia berbagi ketidakpastian pria tentang *baik* bahasa Prancis tingkat pemula maupun menengah, bukan bahwa dia tahu dia belum siap untuk tingkat menengah.

options.text: She wants to take neither beginning nor intermediate French.

reason.en: The phrase 'Neither am I' is an agreement with the man's uncertainty about taking *either* course. It does not mean she wants to take neither course.
reason.id: Frasa 'Neither am I' adalah persetujuan dengan ketidakpastian pria tentang mengambil *salah satu* kursus. Itu tidak berarti dia tidak ingin mengambil kursus sama sekali.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement

questions.passage:
(man) The food in the cafeteria is not exactly the best food I have ever tasted.
(woman) You can say that again!
questions.question: 4. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The man should repeat what he said.

reason.en: The woman's response 'You can say that again!' is an idiom expressing strong agreement, not a literal request for repetition.
reason.id: Respons wanita 'You can say that again!' adalah idiom yang mengekspresikan persetujuan kuat, bukan permintaan harfiah untuk mengulang.

options.text: The man said something foolish.

reason.en: The idiom 'You can say that again!' is used to express agreement, not to criticize the man's statement as foolish.
reason.id: Idiom 'You can say that again!' digunakan untuk mengekspresikan persetujuan, bukan untuk mengkritik pernyataan pria itu sebagai bodoh.

options.text: She thinks that the food is the best she has ever tasted.

reason.en: The woman's statement is in direct agreement with the man's negative comment about the food, meaning she also finds it bad.
reason.id: Pernyataan wanita itu adalah persetujuan langsung dengan komentar negatif pria tentang makanan, yang berarti dia juga menganggapnya buruk.

options.text: She agrees that the food is pretty bad.

reason.en: The idiom 'You can say that again!' is a common expression used to show strong agreement with a previous statement. The man stated the food was not good, and the woman is agreeing with him.
reason.id: Idiom 'You can say that again!' adalah ungkapan umum yang digunakan untuk menunjukkan persetujuan kuat dengan pernyataan sebelumnya. Pria itu menyatakan makanannya tidak enak, dan wanita itu setuju dengannya.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement

questions.passage:
(woman) This party certainly has been fun!
(man) I'll say!
questions.question: 5. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: This party hasn't been any fun at all.

reason.en: The man's response 'I'll say!' is an expression of strong agreement, meaning he agrees enthusiastically. Option [0] states the opposite.
reason.id: Respons pria 'I'll say!' adalah ekspresi persetujuan yang kuat, yang berarti dia setuju dengan antusias. Pilihan [0] menyatakan sebaliknya.

options.text: He wonders if the woman enjoyed herself.

reason.en: The man's response 'I'll say!' is a statement of agreement, not a question about the woman's enjoyment. Option [1] misinterprets the function of the expression.
reason.id: Respons pria 'I'll say!' adalah pernyataan persetujuan, bukan pertanyaan tentang kesenangan wanita. Pilihan [1] salah menafsirkan fungsi ekspresi tersebut.

options.text: He wants to know what she said.

reason.en: The man's response 'I'll say!' is an affirmation of his own feelings about the party, not a request for the woman to repeat herself. Option [2] is irrelevant.
reason.id: Respons pria 'I'll say!' adalah penegasan perasaannya sendiri tentang pesta, bukan permintaan agar wanita mengulang perkataannya. Pilihan [2] tidak relevan.

options.text: He's enjoyed himself tremendously.

reason.en: The expression 'I'll say!' is an idiom used to express strong agreement or emphasis. In this context, it means the man strongly agrees with the woman that the party has been fun, implying he has enjoyed himself tremendously. This aligns with Skill 11 which covers expressions of agreement.
reason.id: Ekspresi 'I'll say!' adalah idiom yang digunakan untuk menyatakan persetujuan atau penekanan yang kuat. Dalam konteks ini, artinya pria tersebut sangat setuju dengan wanita bahwa pesta itu menyenangkan, yang menyiratkan bahwa dia sangat menikmati dirinya sendiri. Hal ini sejalan dengan Skill 11 yang mencakup ekspresi persetujuan.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement

questions.passage:
(man) I'm not about to condone what she did!
(woman) I'm not either.
questions.question: 6. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She condones what happened.

reason.en: The woman explicitly states 'I'm not either,' which is the opposite of condoning. This option contradicts her statement.
reason.id: Wanita secara eksplisit menyatakan 'Saya juga tidak', yang merupakan kebalikan dari menyetujui. Pilihan ini bertentangan dengan pernyataannya.

options.text: She does not like what the man said.

reason.en: The woman's statement is about agreeing with the man's sentiment regarding 'what she did,' not about his specific words or tone.
reason.id: Pernyataan wanita adalah tentang menyetujui sentimen pria mengenai 'apa yang dia lakukan', bukan tentang kata-kata atau nada spesifiknya.

options.text: She agrees with the man about what happened.

reason.en: The phrase 'I'm not either' is an expression of agreement with the man's negative statement ('I'm not about to condone'). This means she also does not condone what she did.
reason.id: Frasa 'Saya juga tidak' adalah ekspresi persetujuan dengan pernyataan negatif pria ('Saya tidak akan menyetujui'). Ini berarti dia juga tidak menyetujui apa yang dia lakukan.

options.text: She says that she did not do it.

reason.en: The dialogue is about the woman's (the speaker's) opinion on someone else's actions ('what she did'), not about her own actions.
reason.id: Dialog ini adalah tentang pendapat wanita (pembicara) tentang tindakan orang lain ('apa yang dia lakukan'), bukan tentang tindakannya sendiri.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement

questions.passage:
(woman) Those people upstairs always have such loud parties.
(man) Don't they!
questions.question: 7. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He thinks the parties aren't loud.

reason.en: The man's response 'Don't they!' is an expression of agreement, meaning he agrees with the woman's statement that the parties are loud. This option states the opposite.
reason.id: Respons pria 'Don't they!' adalah ekspresi persetujuan, yang berarti dia setuju dengan pernyataan wanita bahwa pesta-pesta itu berisik. Pilihan ini menyatakan sebaliknya.

options.text: He says that the neighbors don't have many parties.

reason.en: The man's response 'Don't they!' is an expression of agreement with the woman's statement about the loudness of the parties. This option discusses the frequency of parties, which is not the focus of the man's agreement.
reason.id: Respons pria 'Don't they!' adalah ekspresi persetujuan dengan pernyataan wanita tentang kebisingan pesta. Pilihan ini membahas frekuensi pesta, yang bukan fokus persetujuan pria tersebut.

options.text: He agrees that the upstairs neighbors are noisy.

reason.en: The phrase 'Don't they!' is a common way to express agreement with a negative statement. The woman made a negative statement ('Don't they always have such loud parties'), and the man's response indicates he agrees with her observation that the neighbors are noisy.
reason.id: Frasa 'Don't they!' adalah cara umum untuk menyatakan persetujuan dengan pernyataan negatif. Wanita itu membuat pernyataan negatif ('Bukankah mereka selalu mengadakan pesta yang begitu keras'), dan respons pria itu menunjukkan bahwa dia setuju dengan pengamatannya bahwa tetangga-tetangga itu berisik.

options.text: The loud parties don't bother him.

reason.en: The man's response 'Don't they!' is an expression of agreement with the woman's statement about the loudness of the parties. This option suggests he is not bothered, which is not directly implied by his agreement.
reason.id: Respons pria 'Don't they!' adalah ekspresi persetujuan dengan pernyataan wanita tentang kebisingan pesta. Pilihan ini menyarankan bahwa dia tidak terganggu, yang tidak secara langsung tersirat dari persetujuannya.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement

questions.passage:
(man) This meal is really delicious!
(woman) Isn't it!
questions.question: 8. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She doesn't like this meal too much.

reason.en: The woman uses an affirmative question ('Isn't it!') to agree with the man's positive statement ('This meal is really delicious!'). This option states the opposite of agreement.
reason.id: Wanita itu menggunakan pertanyaan afirmatif ('Isn't it!') untuk menyetujui pernyataan positif pria ('This meal is really delicious!'). Pilihan ini menyatakan kebalikan dari persetujuan.

options.text: This food tastes wonderful to her.

reason.en: The woman uses an affirmative question ('Isn't it!') to agree with the man's positive statement ('This meal is really delicious!'). This implies she also finds the meal wonderful.
reason.id: Wanita itu menggunakan pertanyaan afirmatif ('Isn't it!') untuk menyetujui pernyataan positif pria ('This meal is really delicious!'). Ini menyiratkan bahwa dia juga menganggap makanan itu luar biasa.

options.text: She's not sure if she likes it.

reason.en: The woman uses an affirmative question ('Isn't it!') to agree with the man's positive statement ('This meal is really delicious!'). This option suggests uncertainty, which is contrary to the expression of agreement.
reason.id: Wanita itu menggunakan pertanyaan afirmatif ('Isn't it!') untuk menyetujui pernyataan positif pria ('This meal is really delicious!'). Pilihan ini menunjukkan ketidakpastian, yang bertentangan dengan ekspresi persetujuan.

options.text: She can't stand this meal.

reason.en: The woman uses an affirmative question ('Isn't it!') to agree with the man's positive statement ('This meal is really delicious!'). This option states strong dislike, which is the opposite of agreement.
reason.id: Wanita itu menggunakan pertanyaan afirmatif ('Isn't it!') untuk menyetujui pernyataan positif pria ('This meal is really delicious!'). Pilihan ini menyatakan ketidaksukaan yang kuat, yang merupakan kebalikan dari persetujuan.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement

questions.passage:
(man) I can't imagine what he was thinking about when he bought that car.
(woman) Neither can I!
questions.question: 9. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She agrees that getting the car was not a good idea.

reason.en: The woman uses the phrase 'Neither can I!' which is an expression of agreement. It means she shares the man's sentiment that the purchase of the car was questionable. This aligns with the idea that she agrees it was not a good idea.
reason.id: Wanita menggunakan frasa 'Neither can I!' yang merupakan ekspresi persetujuan. Ini berarti dia berbagi sentimen pria itu bahwa pembelian mobil itu dipertanyakan. Hal ini sejalan dengan gagasan bahwa dia setuju bahwa itu bukanlah ide yang bagus.

options.text: She imagines that she would like to have a similar car.

reason.en: The phrase 'Neither can I!' indicates agreement with the man's disbelief or questioning of the purchase, not a desire to own a similar car.
reason.id: Frasa 'Neither can I!' menunjukkan persetujuan dengan ketidakpercayaan atau pertanyaan pria itu tentang pembelian tersebut, bukan keinginan untuk memiliki mobil serupa.

options.text: She thinks that the man is mistaken about the car.

reason.en: The woman's statement 'Neither can I!' expresses agreement with the man's thought process, not a disagreement or a belief that he is mistaken.
reason.id: Pernyataan wanita 'Neither can I!' mengungkapkan persetujuan dengan proses berpikir pria itu, bukan ketidaksepakatan atau keyakinan bahwa dia salah.

options.text: She thinks the man has no imagination.

reason.en: The phrase 'Neither can I!' is an expression of agreement with the man's sentiment about the car purchase, not a comment on his imagination.
reason.id: Frasa 'Neither can I!' adalah ekspresi persetujuan dengan sentimen pria itu tentang pembelian mobil, bukan komentar tentang imajinasinya.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement

questions.passage:
(woman) This should be a two-semester course.
(man) One semester is just not enough time to learn all the material.
questions.question: 10. You can say that again! WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He would like the woman to repeat what she said.

reason.en: The man is not asking the woman to repeat herself. The idiom 'You can say that again!' means strong agreement.
reason.id: Pria itu tidak meminta wanita itu mengulang perkataannya. Idiom 'You can say that again!' berarti persetujuan yang kuat.

options.text: He thinks that one semester is enough time for the course.

reason.en: The man's statement 'One semester is just not enough time to learn all the material' directly contradicts this option. He disagrees with the idea that one semester is enough.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria itu 'Satu semester saja tidak cukup waktu untuk mempelajari semua materi' secara langsung bertentangan dengan pilihan ini. Dia tidak setuju dengan gagasan bahwa satu semester sudah cukup.

options.text: He also thinks that the course should be extended.

reason.en: The idiom 'You can say that again!' is used to express strong agreement. The man agrees with the woman's implied statement that the course should be longer than one semester, as he explicitly states that one semester is not enough time.
reason.id: Idiom 'You can say that again!' digunakan untuk mengekspresikan persetujuan yang kuat. Pria itu setuju dengan pernyataan tersirat wanita bahwa kursus tersebut seharusnya lebih lama dari satu semester, karena dia secara eksplisit menyatakan bahwa satu semester tidak cukup waktu.

options.text: He would like to take the course two semesters from now.

reason.en: The dialogue is about the duration of a course, not about when the man wants to take it.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut adalah tentang durasi kursus, bukan tentang kapan pria itu ingin mengikutinya.

parts.title: SKILL 12: LISTEN FOR EXPRESSIONS OF UNCERTAINTY AND SUGGESTION
parts.instruction: TOEFL EXERCISE 12: In this exercise, listen carefully to each short dialogue and question on the recording, and then choose the best answer to the question. You should be particularly careful of expressions of uncertainty and suggestion.

questions.passage:
(woman) Are we supposed to read all ten chap ters before the exam?
(man) As far as I can tell, we are.
questions.question: 1. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He's sure about which chapters they are to read.

reason.en: The man does not express certainty about the chapters, only about the assignment itself. The phrase 'As far as I can tell' indicates uncertainty about the specifics, not the overall task.
reason.id: Pria itu tidak mengungkapkan kepastian tentang bab-babnya, hanya tentang tugas itu sendiri. Frasa 'Sejauh yang saya tahu' menunjukkan ketidakpastian tentang spesifikasinya, bukan tugas keseluruhannya.

options.text: He thinks he knows what the assignment is.

reason.en: The man's response, 'As far as I can tell, we are,' directly addresses the woman's question about the reading assignment for the exam, indicating he believes he understands what is required.
reason.id: Tanggapan pria itu, 'Sejauh yang saya tahu, kita harus,' secara langsung menjawab pertanyaan wanita tentang tugas membaca untuk ujian, yang menunjukkan bahwa dia percaya dia mengerti apa yang dibutuhkan.

options.text: He has to tell her how far she should go.

reason.en: The man is not telling the woman how far she should read; he is responding to her question about the scope of the reading assignment.
reason.id: Pria itu tidak memberi tahu wanita itu sejauh mana dia harus membaca; dia menanggapi pertanyaannya tentang cakupan tugas membaca.

options.text: The professor told them to read the chapters after the exam.

reason.en: The man's statement implies the reading is for the exam, not after it. The phrase 'before the exam' in the woman's question sets the context.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria itu menyiratkan bahwa membaca adalah untuk ujian, bukan setelahnya. Frasa 'sebelum ujian' dalam pertanyaan wanita menetapkan konteksnya.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement

questions.passage:
(man) You're out of apple pie!
(woman) Sorry. Why not try the cherry pie?
questions.question: 2. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN SUG GEST?

options.text: The man should take the pie out.

reason.en: The woman does not suggest taking the pie out. She suggests trying the cherry pie as an alternative.
reason.id: Wanita tersebut tidak menyarankan untuk mengeluarkan pai. Dia menyarankan untuk mencoba pai ceri sebagai alternatif.

options.text: The man should try something else.

reason.en: The woman suggests trying the cherry pie because the apple pie is unavailable. This implies the man should try something else.
reason.id: Wanita tersebut menyarankan untuk mencoba pai ceri karena pai apel tidak tersedia. Ini menyiratkan bahwa pria itu harus mencoba sesuatu yang lain.

options.text: The man shouldn't try cherry pie.

reason.en: The woman suggests the cherry pie, implying it is a good alternative, not something the man should avoid.
reason.id: Wanita tersebut menyarankan pai ceri, menyiratkan bahwa itu adalah alternatif yang baik, bukan sesuatu yang harus dihindari pria itu.

options.text: The man should feel sorry.

reason.en: While the woman says 'Sorry,' it is an apology for the lack of apple pie, not a suggestion for the man to feel sorry.
reason.id: Meskipun wanita itu berkata 'Maaf,' itu adalah permintaan maaf atas kurangnya pai apel, bukan saran agar pria itu merasa bersalah.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 12: Listen for Expressions of Uncertainty and Suggestion

questions.passage:
(woman) Do you know when the movie starts?
(man) It starts at 8:00, doesn't it?
questions.question: 3. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He knows the movie starts at 8:00.

reason.en: The man uses a tag question ('doesn't it?') which indicates uncertainty, not certainty. He is seeking confirmation, not stating a fact he knows.
reason.id: Pria itu menggunakan pertanyaan tag ('doesn't it?') yang menunjukkan ketidakpastian, bukan kepastian. Dia mencari konfirmasi, bukan menyatakan fakta yang dia ketahui.

options.text: He is not quite sure when the movie begins.

reason.en: The man uses a tag question ('doesn't it?'). Tag questions are used to express uncertainty and to seek confirmation from the listener. This implies he is not completely sure about the movie's start time.
reason.id: Pria itu menggunakan pertanyaan tag ('doesn't it?'). Pertanyaan tag digunakan untuk mengekspresikan ketidakpastian dan mencari konfirmasi dari pendengar. Ini menyiratkan bahwa dia tidak sepenuhnya yakin tentang waktu mulai film.

options.text: He thinks the start of the movie has been changed.

reason.en: There is no indication in the dialogue that the man thinks the start time has been changed. The tag question is about the stated time of 8:00.
reason.id: Tidak ada indikasi dalam dialog bahwa pria itu berpikir waktu mulai telah diubah. Pertanyaan tag adalah tentang waktu yang disebutkan yaitu 8:00.

options.text: He will start the movie himself at 8:00.

reason.en: The dialogue is about the movie's start time, not about the man starting the movie himself. The phrase 'starts at 8:00' refers to the movie's schedule.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut adalah tentang waktu mulai film, bukan tentang pria itu sendiri yang memulai film. Frasa 'starts at 8:00' mengacu pada jadwal film.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 12: Listen for Expressions of Uncertainty and Suggestion

questions.passage:
(woman) We can't leave now. We have to do the dishes.
(man) Let's leave the dishes until later.
questions.question: 4. WHAT DOES THE MAN SUGGEST?

options.text: Not doing the dishes now .

reason.en: The man's suggestion is 'Let's leave the dishes until later,' which directly implies not doing the dishes now.
reason.id: Saran pria adalah 'Mari kita tinggalkan cucian piring nanti,' yang secara langsung menyiratkan untuk tidak mencuci piring sekarang.

options.text: Leaving the house with the dishes .

reason.en: The man suggests leaving the dishes, not leaving the house with them.
reason.id: Pria itu menyarankan untuk meninggalkan cucian piring, bukan meninggalkan rumah bersamanya.

options.text: Leaving later so that they can do the dishes now .

reason.en: The man suggests leaving the dishes until later, which is the opposite of leaving later to do them now.
reason.id: Pria itu menyarankan untuk meninggalkan cucian piring nanti, yang merupakan kebalikan dari pergi nanti untuk melakukannya sekarang.

options.text: Washing the dishes before they leave .

reason.en: The man suggests leaving the dishes until later, directly contradicting the idea of washing them before they leave.
reason.id: Pria itu menyarankan untuk meninggalkan cucian piring nanti, yang secara langsung bertentangan dengan gagasan mencucinya sebelum mereka pergi.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 12: Listen for Expressions of Uncertainty and Suggestion

questions.passage:
(man) Do you think Matt has enough talent for the role?
(woman) As far as I can tell.
questions.question: 5. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She's told Matt he'll go far.

reason.en: The woman's statement 'As far as I can tell' is an expression of agreement, not a direct statement about Matt's future success. This option introduces information not present in the dialogue.

parts.title: SKILL 13: LISTEN FOR EMPHATIC EXPRESSIONS OF SURPRISE
parts.instruction: TOEFL EXERCISE 13: In this exercise, listen carefully to each short dialogue and question on the recording, and then choose the best answer to the question. You should be particularly careful of expressions of emphatic surprise.

questions.passage:
(man)         Greg should be here any moment.
(woman)       Then, he is coming to the party!
questions.question: 1.       WHAT HAD THE WOMAN ASSUMED?

options.text: Greg always comes to parties.

reason.en: The woman's statement 'Then, he is coming to the party!' is a reaction to the man's statement that Greg should be there soon. It does not imply anything about Greg's usual attendance at parties.
reason.id: Pernyataan wanita 'Kalau begitu, dia datang ke pesta!' adalah reaksi terhadap pernyataan pria bahwa Greg seharusnya segera ada di sana. Pernyataan itu tidak menyiratkan apa pun tentang kehadiran Greg yang biasa di pesta.

options.text: Greg would come to the party later.

reason.en: The woman's surprise indicates she did not expect Greg to come at all, or at least not at this time. Her statement implies the opposite of him coming later.
reason.id: Kejutan wanita itu menunjukkan bahwa dia tidak menduga Greg akan datang sama sekali, atau setidaknya tidak pada waktu ini. Pernyataannya menyiratkan kebalikan dari dia datang nanti.

options.text: Greg was unable to attend the party.

reason.en: The woman's surprised tone and statement 'Then, he is coming to the party!' suggest that she previously believed Greg would not be able to attend. The man's statement that Greg should be there soon contradicts her prior assumption.
reason.id: Nada terkejut wanita itu dan pernyataannya 'Kalau begitu, dia datang ke pesta!' menunjukkan bahwa dia sebelumnya percaya Greg tidak akan bisa hadir. Pernyataan pria bahwa Greg seharusnya segera ada di sana bertentangan dengan asumsi sebelumnya.

options.text: Greg would stay at the party for only a moment.

reason.en: The dialogue focuses on whether Greg is coming to the party, not on how long he will stay. Her surprise is about his attendance, not the duration.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut berfokus pada apakah Greg datang ke pesta, bukan pada berapa lama dia akan tinggal. Kejutannya adalah tentang kehadirannya, bukan durasinya.

Frasa 'Kalau begitu, kamu akan mengikuti tes sore ini' adalah reaksi terhadap studinya, menyiratkan kontras dengan asumsi sebelumnya.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement

questions.passage:

Ini berarti dia berasumsi bahwa pipa-pipa tersebut *tidak* bersih sebelum tukang ledeng berkunjung. Kejutan dalam nada suaranya menunjukkan bahwa ini adalah hasil yang diharapkan dari kunjungan tukang ledeng.

options.text: The plumber would be late.

reason.en: There is no information in the dialogue to suggest the man assumed the plumber would be late.
reason.id: Tidak ada informasi dalam dialog yang menunjukkan pria itu berasumsi tukang ledeng akan terlambat.

options.text: The plumber had already cleared the pipes.

reason.en: The man's statement implies the opposite: that the pipes were *not* clear before the plumber's visit and that the plumber's job was to clear them.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria itu menyiratkan sebaliknya: bahwa pipa-pipa tersebut *tidak* bersih sebelum kunjungan tukang ledeng dan bahwa pekerjaan tukang ledeng adalah membersihkannya.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 13: Listen for Emphatic Expressions of Surprise

sections.instruction: Expressions of agreement are common in the short dialogues, so you should become familiar with them. The following example shows agreement with a positive statement.

questions.passage:
(man) The phone bill is certainly high this month!
(woman) I'll say!
questions.question: 1. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She plans to talk a lot this month.

reason.en: The woman's statement 'I'll say!' is an expression of agreement, not a statement about her future phone usage.
reason.id: Pernyataan wanita 'I'll say!' adalah ekspresi persetujuan, bukan pernyataan tentang penggunaan teleponnya di masa depan.

options.text: She has a lot to say about the phone bill.

reason.en: While the woman might have a lot to say, her phrase 'I'll say!' in this context specifically means agreement with the man's observation about the high phone bill.
reason.id: Meskipun wanita itu mungkin banyak bicara, frasa 'I'll say!' dalam konteks ini secara khusus berarti persetujuan dengan pengamatan pria tentang tagihan telepon yang tinggi.

options.text: The bill is high because she has a lot to say.

reason.en: The woman's agreement is with the man's statement about the bill being high, not a cause-and-effect relationship where her talking caused the high bill.
reason.id: Persetujuan wanita itu adalah dengan pernyataan pria bahwa tagihan itu tinggi, bukan hubungan sebab-akibat di mana perkataannya menyebabkan tagihan tinggi.

options.text: She agrees with the man.

reason.en: The expression 'I'll say!' is a common idiom used to express strong agreement with a previous statement. The woman is agreeing with the man that the phone bill is high.
reason.id: Ungkapan 'I'll say!' adalah idiom umum yang digunakan untuk menyatakan persetujuan kuat dengan pernyataan sebelumnya. Wanita itu setuju dengan pria bahwa tagihan telepon itu tinggi.
